section_header: Letter from the MAYOR
policy_statement: Invest $188 million in equity-focused climate interventions, including building retrofits, expansion of the air quality monitoring network, and planting over 75,000 trees.
verbatim_text: To kickstart the implementation of my administration's climate strategy, we are investing $188 million in equity-focused interventions, spanning building retrofits, the expansion of the air quality monitoring network, to the planting of over 75,000 trees.
extraction_rationale: Explicit resource allocation ($188 million) tied to specific interventions; qualifies as a policy commitment.

section_header: TREE EQUITY
policy_statement: Plant 75,000 trees by 2026.
verbatim_text: Together with city departments and community partners, Chicago has the goal of planting 75,000 trees by 2026.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline (75,000 trees by 2026).

section_header: GHG REDUCTION TARGETS / ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
policy_statement: Reduce Chicago's GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040 based on 2017 inventory levels.
verbatim_text:
The 2022 CAP aims to chart an equitable path to reduce Chicago's GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040.

Guided by the strategies and actions of this plan, the City of Chicago aims to reduce the city's level of GHG emissions by 62% by 2040, based on 2017 GHG inventory levels.
extraction_rationale: Quantifiable economy-wide emissions reduction target with year.

section_header: The Role of Offsets
policy_statement: Do not count carbon offset credits toward Chicago’s 62% GHG reduction goal; prioritize reducing the City's own emissions before action beyond its borders.
verbatim_text: Chicago will not count offset credits toward its 62% GHG reduction goal achievement. Furthermore, the City will reduce its own emissions before taking action beyond its borders.
extraction_rationale: Clear policy rule restricting use of offsets for meeting the target.

section_header: CLIMATE FINANCING AND DELIVERY CAPACITY
policy_statement: Benchmarking Ordinance (2013) requiring covered buildings (50,000 sq ft and larger) to track and annually report energy use to the City and verify data accuracy every three years; expanded in 2019 via Chicago Energy Rating System requiring on-site display of ratings.
verbatim_text: Benchmarking Ordinance, 2013: This ordinance supports greater transparency on energy usage across the building sector. Existing municipal, commercial, and residential buildings that are 50,000 square feet and larger are called to track energy use, report to the City annually, and verify data accuracy every three years. Chicago's benchmarking program was expanded in 2019 with the Chicago Energy Rating System, which assigns an energy rating to covered buildings based on their reporting and requires buildings to display the rating in a prominent location onsite.
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (ordinance) with clear requirements and thresholds.

section_header: CLIMATE FINANCING AND DELIVERY CAPACITY
policy_statement: EV Readiness Ordinance (2020) requiring specified new buildings to provide 20% of parking spaces as EV-ready (5+ unit residential with on-site parking; non-residential with 30+ spaces).
verbatim_text: EV Readiness Ordinance, 2020: This ordinance requires new construction of a residential building with 5 or more units and on-site parking to have 20% of parking spaces EV Ready, and in non-residential buildings with 30 or more parking spaces to provide 20% of parking spaces as EV ready.
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (ordinance) with explicit percentage requirements and applicability thresholds.

section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan
policy_statement: Chicago Recovery Plan climate investment: Decarbonize Affordable Multifamily Buildings program to retrofit and decarbonize 200 multifamily affordable housing units in underserved communities using $6 million.
verbatim_text: Decarbonize Affordable Multifamily Buildings | $6 million | Retrofit and decarbonize 200 multifamily affordable housing units in underserved communities.
extraction_rationale: Resource allocation ($6M) with quantified deliverable (200 units).

extraction_rationale: Resource allocation ($15M) with quantified deliverable (250 homes).

extraction_rationale: Budgeted program with quantified deliverable (75,000 trees) and timeframe (5 years).
